answers.value:
We are a small boutique interior design studio (Lumen & Loft Interiors) based in Portland, Oregon, and this will be our primary marketing and client acquisition website. Beyond the options already listed, we need a few additional pieces of functionality:

1. Project Inquiry Questionnaire & Workflow
We want a more in-depth, multi-step project inquiry form specifically tailored to interior design projects. This should go beyond a simple contact form and include:
- Basic contact details
- Type of property (apartment, single-family home, office, retail, etc.)
- Scope of work (single room refresh, full home, kitchen remodel, commercial space, etc.)
- Approximate square footage
- Budget range and ideal timeline
- Style preferences (with checkboxes and space for open-ended answers)
- File upload field for floor plans, inspiration photos, and existing room photos

On submission, this should:
- Automatically send the prospect a confirmation email with a summary of their responses and a link to schedule a discovery call (we’re currently using Calendly but are open to other tools).
- Tag and store the lead in our CRM (we use HubSpot) with the relevant metadata (budget range, project type, etc.).

2. Design Package Comparison Tool
We offer three main service tiers (Consultation, Full-Service Design, and E-Design). We’d like a comparison section where potential clients can:
- See a side-by-side comparison table of inclusions and pricing ranges.
- Expand each package to view a more detailed breakdown.
- Click a “Start This Package” button that takes them directly into a pre-filtered version of the inquiry form (e.g., automatically indicating which package they’re interested in).

3. Client Portal Entry Point
We already use a separate client portal tool (Studio Designer), but we’d like a simple, password-protected link or button in the main navigation labeled “Client Login.” This should:
- Lead to a simple login screen or at least a secure link redirect to the external portal.
- Be clearly separated from our general public content so only active clients use it.

4. Testimonial / Case Study Layouts
We want flexible page templates to highlight detailed case studies of past projects, including:
- Before/after slider for key spaces.
- Short project overview (location, type, timeline, budget range).
- Client testimonial quote with photo and name (where clients have given permission).
- A small “Design Challenges & Solutions” section with icons or visual callouts.

5. Simple Appointment Scheduling Integration
In addition to linking out to Calendly, we’d like an embedded scheduler on key pages (e.g., “Work With Us” and possibly the homepage) so visitors don’t have to leave the site. Ideally, this would:
- Allow users to pick a time for a 20–30 minute discovery call.
- Sync automatically with our Google Workspace calendars.

6. GDPR / Privacy & Cookie Management
We do occasionally have European clients and newsletter subscribers, so we’d like:
- A clear cookie consent banner with options to accept or manage preferences.
- A simple way for users to request deletion of their data via a form.

7. SEO & Blog Enhancements
For our blog/content area, we’d like a few slightly more advanced features:
- Ability to organize posts by category and tag (e.g., “Small Space Solutions,” “Before & After,” “Designer Tips”).
- Built-in SEO fields for each page and post (meta title, description, alt text prompts, etc.).
- Related posts or “You may also like” section at the bottom of each blog article.

8. Lead Magnet Delivery
We plan to offer downloadable resources like a “Pre-Renovation Checklist” and “Guide to Lighting Your Living Room.” We’d like:
- A simple gated content system where users enter their name and email to access the download.
- Automatic email delivery of the PDF plus adding the user to a specific mailing list segment in our email marketing tool (we use Mailchimp).

If anything above needs to be simplified for phase one, we’d prioritize the advanced inquiry form, design package comparison, embedded scheduling, and email segmenting for lead magnets.

answers.field_label: Systems Our New Website Needs To Integrate With
answers.value:
We need the new website to integrate with the following systems and tools that we already use in our business:

1. HubSpot CRM
- Purpose: Capture and manage leads from all website forms.
- Requirements:
  - New inquiries from the main contact form, project inquiry form, and lead magnet forms should automatically create or update contacts in HubSpot.
  - Custom fields for project type, budget range, and service package selected should be passed into HubSpot.
  - Tagging or list membership based on which form or page the lead came from (e.g., “Website – Full-Service Inquiry,” “Website – E-Design Inquiry,” “Lead Magnet – Lighting Guide”).

2. Mailchimp
- Purpose: Email newsletter and automated sequences for lead magnets.
- Requirements:
  - Email newsletter signup forms on the website should feed directly into our primary Mailchimp audience.
  - Lead magnet opt-ins should add users to specific Mailchimp tags or groups based on the resource they downloaded.
  - Double opt-in support where required.

3. Calendly
- Purpose: Discovery call and consultation scheduling.
- Requirements:
  - Embedded Calendly widget on the “Work With Us” page and potentially the homepage.
  - Links in automated confirmation emails sent after form submissions should point to the relevant Calendly event type.

4. Google Maps
- Purpose: Display our studio location and service area.
- Requirements:
  - Embedded map on the Contact page with our studio address in Portland, OR.

5. Instagram & Pinterest
- Purpose: Showcase our design portfolio and social proof.
- Requirements:
  - Instagram feed integration (grid or carousel) on the homepage and/or portfolio page.
  - Simple Pinterest “Pin It” or share buttons on portfolio images and blog posts.

6. Studio Designer (Client Portal)
- Purpose: External platform we use for project management and client portal access.
- Requirements:
  - A secure “Client Login” button in the main navigation that links to our Studio Designer portal login page (even if this is just a styled external link rather than a deep integration).

If some of these can only be implemented via simple embed codes or API keys (e.g., Calendly, Google Maps, Instagram feed), that’s perfectly fine. We just want to ensure the website is set up so these tools work smoothly together and we’re not manually copying data between systems.
